# doit_insights (Data Source)

Manage cloud insights representing recommendations and findings for cloud resources.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# List all insights (auto-paginates to fetch everything)
data "doit_insights" "all" {
}

output "total_insights" {
  value = data.doit_insights.all.pagination.row_count
}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Filter by category and cloud provider
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

data "doit_insights" "finops_gcp" {
  category       = "FinOps"
  cloud_provider = "gcp"
}

output "gcp_finops_count" {
  value = data.doit_insights.finops_gcp.pagination.row_count
}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Manual pagination with max_results
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

data "doit_insights" "first_page" {
  max_results = 10
}

output "first_page_count" {
  value = length(data.doit_insights.first_page.results)
}

output "next_page_token" {
  value = data.doit_insights.first_page.pagination.page_token
}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Filter by display status and priority
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

data "doit_insights" "actionable_high" {
  display_status = ["actionable"]
  priority       = ["High"]
}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Search by text
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

data "doit_insights" "search" {
  search_term = "idle"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `category` (String) Filter by insight category.
Possible values: `FinOps`, `OperationalExcellence`, `PerformanceEfficiency`, `Reliability`, `Security`, `Sustainability`
- `cloud_flows` (Boolean) When true, return only insights that have associated CloudFlow automations.
- `cloud_provider` (String) Filter by cloud provider (e.g. "aws", "gcp", "azure").
- `display_status` (List of String) Filter by one or more display statuses.
- `easy_win` (Boolean) When true, return only insights flagged as easy wins.
- `max_results` (Number) Maximum number of results per page (default 50, max 500).
- `page_token` (String) Token from a previous response to fetch the next page.
- `priority` (List of String) Filter by one or more priority levels.
- `search_term` (String) Free-text search term to filter insights by title or description.
- `source` (List of String) the source that generated insights
- `tag` (List of String) Filter by one or more tags (e.g. security certification labels like "ISO").
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `pagination` (Attributes) Cursor-based pagination metadata. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--pagination))
- `results` (Attributes List) List of insight results. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--results))

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).


<a id="nestedatt--pagination"></a>
### Nested Schema for `pagination`

Read-Only:

- `page_token` (String) Token to retrieve the next page. Absent when there are no more pages.
- `row_count` (Number) Number of items in this page.


<a id="nestedatt--results"></a>
### Nested Schema for `results`

Read-Only:

- `categories` (List of String) Categories this insight belongs to.
- `cloud_flow_template_id` (String) ID of a CloudFlow template that can automate the remediation of this insight.
- `cloud_provider` (String) The cloud provider associated with the resource.
- `detailed_description_mdx` (String) A detailed description of the insight in MDX format.
- `dismissal_details` (Attributes) Details for why an insight was dismissed. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--results--dismissal_details))
- `display_status` (String) The display status of the insight.
- `easy_win_description` (String) A description of why this insight is considered an easy win.
- `key` (String) The unique key identifying this insight.
- `last_status_change` (Attributes) If set, this object contains the last status change made by a user for this insight (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--results--last_status_change))
- `last_updated` (String) Timestamp of the last update to this insight.
- `report_url` (String) URL to an external report related to this insight.
- `short_description` (String) A brief summary of the insight.
- `source` (String) The source that generated the insight.
- `summary` (Attributes) Aggregate summary of risks and savings across all resource results for an insight. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--results--summary))
- `tags` (List of String) Tags for the insight, primarily used for security certification labels (e.g. ISO).
- `title` (String) The display title of the insight.

<a id="nestedatt--results--dismissal_details"></a>
### Nested Schema for `results.dismissal_details`

Read-Only:

- `comment` (String) An optional free-text comment providing additional context.
- `reason` (String) The reason for dismissal.


<a id="nestedatt--results--last_status_change"></a>
### Nested Schema for `results.last_status_change`

Read-Only:

- `last_changed_at` (String)
- `user_id` (String) the reference to the user who made the change (if it was made by a user) If the change was made by an automated system, this reference is empty.


<a id="nestedatt--results--summary"></a>
### Nested Schema for `results.summary`

Read-Only:

- `operational_risks` (Number) Total number of operational risks.
- `performance_risks` (Number) Total number of performance risks.
- `potential_daily_savings` (Number) Total potential daily savings in USD.
- `reliability_risks` (Number) Total number of reliability risks.
- `security_risks` (Number) Total number of security risks.
- `sustainability_risks` (Number) Total number of sustainability risks.
